pr.title: chore(rules): Developer Walkthrough gate + vertical slice policy
pr.author: vertz-tech-lead

testBeforeImpl.evidence: No code changes — TDD does not apply

redGreenCycle.evidence: No code changes — TDD does not apply

qualityGates.evidence: CI skipped lint/test for docs-only changes (path filters). Not a violation.

process.reason: Clean git process with proper review. Minor: no ticket reference (but acceptable for reactive process work).

conventionalCommits.evidence: All 4 commits use chore(rules): or plan: prefix

atomicCommits.evidence: Work split into 4 logical commits: SSR plan, retrospective, rule updates, Three Laws

cleanDiff.evidence: 4 files changed, all directly related to PR scope (no stray files)

changesetPresent.evidence: No changeset needed (docs-only, no package changes)

botScriptsUsed.evidence: Cannot verify (no session transcript). Commit author format suggests compliance.

worktreeIsolation.evidence: Cannot verify (no session transcript). Clean diff suggests no leakage.

ticketReference.evidence: No ticket ID in PR body or commits. PR says 'Post-mortem from SSR DX gap' but no ticket created.

design.reason: Exemplary retrospective and process design. Clear root cause analysis, concrete solutions.

readTicket.evidence: No ticket (reactive process work). PR body provides full context.

readRules.evidence: Work establishes new rules, demonstrating deep understanding of existing process gaps

scopeMatches.evidence: All changes match stated scope: rules updates + retrospective + SSR plan

deviationsEscalated.evidence: No deviations (this work defines the baseline)

dx.reason: High-quality writing. Establishes Developer Walkthrough requirement that will improve future DX.

developerWalkthrough.evidence: N/A — this PR establishes the Developer Walkthrough requirement (meta-work)

acceptanceCriteria.evidence: New rules include clear, testable criteria for future work

examplesUsePublicAPI.evidence: New policy requires examples use public API only

verticalSlices.evidence: New policy mandates vertical slices with clear wrong/right examples

securityRulesIntact.evidence: New rules don't weaken security (Three Laws reference 0th Law: Security)

violations.description: No ticket reference in PR body or commits
violations.evidence: PR #266 body says 'Post-mortem from the SSR DX gap' but doesn't reference a ticket ID (e.g., process-001). RULES.md states 'Never commit without a ticket.'
violations.recommendation: For future process improvements, either: (1) Create a lightweight ticket for traceability, OR (2) Document in RULES.md that retrospectives triggered by specific incidents can be ticket-less if incident is referenced in PR body.

violations.description: Cannot verify bot scripts were used (no session transcript access)
violations.evidence: Commit author format (vertz-tech-lead[bot]) suggests compliance, but auditor lacks session transcript to confirm git-as.sh and gh-as.sh were used.
violations.recommendation: Provide auditors with session transcript access for full process verification.

summary: High-quality process improvement work. Establishes Developer Walkthrough and vertical slice requirements that will prevent future 'works but unusable' features. Exemplary retrospective with honest root cause analysis and concrete solutions. Clean git process with proper review. Only minor gap: no ticket reference (acceptable for reactive process work, but should be documented as an exception in RULES.md). Grade: B+ (would be A with ticket).
strengths: Honest, specific retrospective identifying 5 distinct failure modes
Concrete, actionable new rules with wrong/right examples
High writing quality — professional, structured, scannable
Clean git process: proper branch naming, atomic commits, conventional commit messages
Clean diff with no scope creep or stray files
Systemic fixes (Developer Walkthrough, vertical slices) will prevent future gaps
recommendations: Create retrospective template based on retro-ssr-dx-gap.md structure
Document when ticket-less PRs are acceptable (e.g., incident-driven process improvements)
Update PR template to remind reviewers to check Developer Walkthrough section for feature PRs
Schedule follow-up audit after zero-config SSR ships (issue #265) to verify new rules work
Track rule adoption: how will you verify future PRs follow Developer Walkthrough requirement?
Measure DX improvement after zero-config SSR: setup time, LOC reduced, error rate
